reviews.author: Filipe Manana
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch does not check if the root is null, which can lead to null pointer dereferences when the file system is corrupted or mounted with the rescue option, and requested adding a null check with an error message.
reviews.raw_body:
Please, check that the root is not null; if it is null, log an error
message and return an error.
We found many missing instances that sometimes triggered null pointer
dereferences when the file system was corrupted or mounted with the
rescue option, among other issues.
See:

https://git.kernel.org/pub/scm/linux/kernel/git/torvalds/linux.git/commit/?id=2b4cb4e58f3463d142fcece5a19e0405fb82c794

Thanks.

2026-04-15.patch_summary: This patch adds a new unprivileged ioctl, BTRFS_IOC_GET_CSUMS, which allows userspace to query the on-disk checksums for a file without exposing raw csum tree lookups. The ioctl takes a struct btrfs_ioctl_get_csums_args detailing the offset and length of interest, and writes a struct btrfs_ioctl_get_csums_entry into a provided buffer followed by the checksums if available. This is designed to speed up mkfs.btrfs --rootdir operations, particularly when using the --reflink option, by avoiding recalculation of checksums or reading data entirely.
